findings.title: New optimizer endpoint has zero test coverage
findings.why_it_matters: The entire C22 optimizer endpoint has 7 HTTPException raise sites across 6 validation branches (empty params, too many params, invalid metric, top_n out of range, empty values list, too many values, combo cap), plus two distinct exception-handling paths in the run loop (re-raise on bad param_path vs skip on bad backtest run), plus the ranking/truncation logic. None of these branches are exercised by any test. The project has 18 backend test files covering every other route; this new endpoint has zero. A regression in any guard — e.g. the combo cap arithmetic or skipped counter — goes undetected.
findings.suggested_fix: Add backend/tests/test_backtest_optimizer.py. Use pytest + fastapi TestClient. Minimum cases: (1) happy path 2-param grid returns ranked results; (2) each 400 guard (empty params, >3 params, invalid metric, top_n=0, top_n=51, empty values, values>10, combos>200); (3) skipped counter — patch run_backtest to raise HTTPException on one combo, assert skipped=1 and completed=N-1; (4) _apply_param HTTPException re-raises out of endpoint. Mock run_backtest with a minimal summary dict to avoid real data dependency.
evidence: ls /Users/jroxenhed/Documents/strategylab/backend/tests/ shows 18 test files, none named test_backtest_optimizer.py
backtest_optimizer.py lines 60-82: 7 HTTPException raise sites across 6 input-validation branches, all unreachable by any existing test

Lines 93-94: except HTTPException: raise — re-raises param-path errors; lines 108-109: except HTTPException: skipped += 1 — skips bad-run combos. Both branches have different semantics and neither is tested

findings.title: useTradingQueries adaptiveMs branch untested
findings.why_it_matters: adaptiveMs() is the core of F19s adaptive polling strategy: when any broker is unhealthy it clamps to 10s regardless of normalMs. If this branch breaks — e.g. the broker query cache key changes from the string bracket literal or the health field shape changes — all five polling hooks silently revert to their normal fast intervals during broker outages, reversing the intent. The project has useOHLCV.test.ts as the established hook test pattern, but useTradingQueries.ts has no test file.
findings.suggested_fix: Add frontend/src/test/useTradingQueries.test.ts. Test adaptiveMs in isolation by priming a QueryClient with synthetic broker data and calling the function with known normalMs. Cases: broker cache absent returns normalMs; all brokers healthy returns normalMs; one broker healthy=false returns max(normalMs, 10000). For the hook exports, verify refetchInterval is a function (not a constant) and that it delegates to adaptiveMs with the correct normalMs for each hook.
evidence: grep -rn useTradingQueries frontend/src/test/ returned no output
adaptiveMs lines 10-14: reads [bracket]broker[bracket] from queryClient cache, branches on anyUnhealthy — two distinct code paths with no test
useOHLCV.test.ts has 8 test cases covering hook behavior with mocked fetch — the pattern is established but not applied to the new file

findings.title: Inner catch in optimizer run loop too narrow
findings.why_it_matters: Line 108 catches only HTTPException to increment the skipped counter. If run_backtest raises a plain Exception — a pandas ValueError for a degenerate param combination (e.g. RSI period=0 producing NaN arrays), a NumPy overflow, or any non-HTTP error in the backtest layer — the entire optimize_backtest request terminates with a 500 and partial results are lost. The comment says these are skipped combos, but the catch scope does not match that intent, and no test verifies the actual failure mode.
findings.suggested_fix: Widen the inner catch to `except Exception: skipped += 1` so non-HTTP errors from individual combos are isolated. Add a test: patch run_backtest to raise ValueError on one specific combo, assert response.completed = N-1 and response.skipped = 1. Mark gated because widening could mask programming errors during development — should be explicit decision.
evidence: Line 108: except HTTPException: skipped += 1 — plain Exception propagates, aborting the entire grid run

Comment line 109: # invalid param value for this combo (e.g. RSI period=1) — RSI period=1 may raise ValueError or ZeroDivisionError in the signal engine depending on indicator implementation, not an HTTPException
No test exists to document or verify the failure-isolation contract

findings.title: OptimizerPanel pure helpers untested (linspace, buildParamOptions)
findings.why_it_matters: linspace is the sole value-generation function for all optimizer param grids; its output directly determines what the backend receives. The edge case min>max (user enters max below min) produces a descending sequence that silently passes validation. buildParamOptions branches across stop_loss_pct, trailing_stop, buy_rules, sell_rules and their sub-fields — non-trivial logic that determines which params are available in the UI. Both are pure functions, trivially testable, but have zero coverage.
findings.suggested_fix: Add tests for linspace: (1) normal range produces correct count and boundary values; (2) steps=1 returns [min]; (3) min==max returns constant array; (4) min>max produces descending values (document this as the intended behavior or add a swap guard). For buildParamOptions: verify a StrategyRequest with no stop_loss_pct omits the stop_loss entry; verify a buy_rule with value!=null produces a buy_rule_N_value entry.
evidence: linspace defined at lines 435-441, buildParamOptions at lines 355-433 — both are pure functions with no test file
No reference to linspace, buildParamOptions, or OptimizerPanel found in any file under frontend/src/test/ or frontend/src/features/strategy/
steps<=1 guard at line 436 is the only documented edge case; min>max is silently accepted and produces descending values that flow to the backend

residual_risks: F19 adaptiveMs reads the broker query cache synchronously at refetch-callback time. If broker health flips unhealthy between two refetch cycles, one extra cycle fires at the normal (fast) interval before the callback sees the unhealthy state. Minor, acceptable for a polling UI, but undocumented.

C22 endpoint is synchronous and blocks the FastAPI event loop for the duration of all combo backtests. With 200 combos on slow data this could take 30-60s and block concurrent requests. NEXT_RUN.md acknowledges this; no timeout or streaming mitigation exists yet.

OrderHistory.tsx and PositionsTable.tsx pass onStale as a prop without memoization at the call site. The useEffect that calls onStale depends on the prop reference. A non-stable onStale will re-run the effect on every parent render, potentially calling onStale with the previous data snapshot. NEXT_RUN.md flags this as P3.
testing_gaps: backend/routes/backtest_optimizer.py: No test file. All 7 validation branches, the combo-skipping counter, ranking by metric, top_n truncation, and the exception-boundary semantics (re-raise vs skip) are completely untested.
frontend/src/shared/hooks/useTradingQueries.ts: No test file. The adaptiveMs healthy/unhealthy branch and the refetchInterval configuration of all five exported hooks are untested.
frontend/src/features/strategy/OptimizerPanel.tsx: linspace, buildParamOptions, and colColor are non-trivial pure functions with zero test coverage.
